title: andryala pinnatifida subsp. teydensis
intro: Andryala pinnatifida subsp. teydensis, also known as Teyda's Andryala, is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Andryala pinnatifida subsp. teydensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive issues, and support immune function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or tincture, and it is also used in folk medicine as a treatment for fever, cough, and respiratory issues, although its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied.
